question: The extended Z(4) lattice gauge model in four dimensions is defined by the two-parameter plaquette action S(J1,J2) = J1 * sum_P (1 - Re Tr U_P) + J2 * sum_P (1 - Re Tr U_P^2), where U_P is the ordered product of Z(4)-valued link variables around plaquette P. Heat-bath Monte Carlo simulations are performed on lattices from 4^4 to 8^4. The Z(4) phase diagram has four regions. The parameter region with J2>=0 (above or on the Wilson-action curve X2=X1^2 in the (X1,X2) Boltzmann-weight plane) is studied. What is the ORDER of the phase transitions for the Z(4) lattice gauge model in the regime J2>=0? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: Extended Z(4) lattice gauge model in 4D with two-parameter plaquette action, J2>=0 regime
metadata.source_excerpt: Monte Carlo data indicate that all transitions for J2>=0 (i.e., for models in the region above the Wilson-action curve X2=X1^2 in the (X1,X2) plane) are first order... latent-heat behavior
metadata.difficulty_note: The first-order character weakens as one moves from the Potts point toward the bifurcation; below the Wilson curve (J2<0) the picture changes and a massless/continuous phase may appear.
